title: MANGA Darkhorse licensed Evangelion Comic Tribute Manga
display_title: MANGA Darkhorse licensed Evangelion Comic Tribute Manga
wikitext:
[]: [MANGA] Darkhorse licensed Evangelion: Comic Tribute Manga             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Apr 07, 2012 6:01 pm http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2012-04-07/dark-horse-adds-oreimo-blood-c-evangelion/comic-tribute-manga Thanks to Vito in IRC for the link. I've read some of Tony Takezaki's Eva manga published in Shounen Ace or Young Ace before, so this should be hilarious.

[]:             Quote Post by cyharding » Sat Apr 07, 2012 7:44 pm That's good to know. Is this the same gag manga that was mentioned in the back of the US edition of volume 8 of Sadamoto's manga? It's been a good while since I've read it and my copy is in a box that's not too easy to get to.

[]:             Quote Post by ONIAgent150 » Sun Apr 08, 2012 2:57 am Exciting! Is it all parody? Some of those artists suggest it won't be all laughs.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sun Apr 08, 2012 8:19 pm Ask Eva Monkey. I'm 95% sure I picked up a copy of this for him last time I was in Japan (in the fall of 2010!)

[]:             Quote Post by Final Messenger » Sun Apr 08, 2012 9:11 pm this should prove to be entertaining

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Sun Apr 08, 2012 11:29 pm drinian wrote: Ask Eva Monkey. I'm 95% sure I picked up a copy of this for him last time I was in Japan (in the fall of 2010!)

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Tue Apr 10, 2012 4:23 pm http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/11676/What-on-earth-is-this/ It's this in case anyone was wondering.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Mar 18, 2013 10:01 pm Anybody have this (in English)? It's been available for about a month: http://www.amazon.com/Neon-Genesis-Evangelion-Comic-Tribute/dp/1616551143/ I'm curious just how funny it is.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Mar 19, 2013 6:09 am Implying that it's not sad ; I still have to order it anyway, I noticed that they already published it just a pair of days ago.

[]:             Quote Post by hui43210 » Tue Mar 19, 2013 9:38 am I've got it, read it. Its pretty funny overall, there's one that's a very good parody of ISRK and its "brand" of stoytelling. Also, the ones by the Sgt frog guy are very funny. There are 2-3 stories I found fairly touching as well. Edit; oh I just noticed Hyper posted the story I found the most touching/sad.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Mar 19, 2013 4:49 pm So if I understood it correctly, the sad/touching one is set during Instrumentality(the folding chair) or in the period between his coming back from the LCL sea and his reuniting with Asuka, I'm right?

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Tue Mar 19, 2013 8:04 pm Have it but have not gone too far. The one complaint so far would be that the font is smaller than expected. So far the piece involving Fuyutsuki gathering the pilots to remedy people's opinion of Nerv was hilarious (They're the Illuminati, yo. )

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Apr 10, 2013 1:27 pm Finished this last weekend. I laughed so hard I cried at some points. It's a great manga, except maybe for the fact that one of the comics was drawn by a hentai artist. That was awkward to discover.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Fri Apr 12, 2013 10:10 am Finally finished it last week too. Overall a good pickup for Eva fans. Who is the artist in question and what story did they do?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Apr 12, 2013 11:37 am AR-99 wrote: Finally finished it last week too. Overall a good pickup for Eva fans. Who is the artist in question and what story did they do?

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Fri Apr 12, 2013 8:34 pm Yup, the story is Let's Study With Nerv!

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Apr 12, 2013 8:38 pm AR-99 wrote: Yup, the story is Let's Study With Nerv!

[]:             Quote Post by steve_stevenson » Sat Apr 13, 2013 6:33 pm cool book the one with the dogs made me lol pretty hard

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Apr 17, 2013 11:06 am I just finished reading it, Yun Kouga's one felt kinda out of place, even though I have to say that Eternal Fourteen was also pretty serious once you notice its context. Most of the other stories were really hilarious, but I kinda missed the point of some Yushi Kawata strips, and I was tempted to throw the manga out of the window when I read the "LAS" one . Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Finished this last weekend. I laughed so hard I cried at some points. It's a great manga, except maybe for the fact that one of the comics was drawn by a hentai artist. That was awkward to discover.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Thu Apr 18, 2013 1:36 pm I think a lot of the guest contributors used this as an excuse to get their fan service on, there's quite a lot of it. Two of my favorites are from whoever did the yonkoma series of 4 panels strips, especially Shinji spraying the cockroach and Seele revealing that they're masochists.
summary:
[]: [MANGA] Darkhorse licensed Evangelion: Comic Tribute Manga             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Apr 07, 2012 6:01 pm http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2012-04-07/dark-horse-adds-oreimo-blood-c-evangelion/comic-tribute-manga Thanks to Vito in IRC for the link. I've read some of Tony Takezaki's Eva manga published in Shounen Ace or Young Ace before, so this should be hilarious.

[]:       &n
